fragility_points: BTC only needs to dip below 72k at one exact minute to resolve No.
A fresh macro or crypto headline could compress the cushion quickly.
key_assumptions: Remaining risk is mostly ordinary short-horizon BTC volatility rather than a special Binance-specific anomaly.
Current cross-venue spot coherence means Binance is not materially off-market.
No major catalyst forces BTC back below 72k by Apr. 15 noon ET.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket rules make Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET one-minute close the governing source of truth.
Current Binance price and recent one-minute candles are safely above 72k.
Independent spot checks suggest Binance is not uniquely elevated.
Therefore Yes is favored, but noon-minute path risk caps confidence.
main_thesis: Yes is likelier than No because Binance BTCUSDT is already materially above 72k, but the contract is narrower than generic BTC direction because it settles on a single noon ET one-minute close on Binance.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified.
source_quality_view: Primary contract and venue sources were strong; contextual spot cross-checks improved confidence. Independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low-to-medium because the rule is clear but single-minute-close contracts remain mechanically fragile.
strongest_disconfirmers: Resolution depends on one exact Binance one-minute close at 12:00 ET, so a modest temporary selloff could still resolve No.
Single-venue settlement creates some operational and divergence risk.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot checked near 73823, about 1823 above strike.
Recent Binance one-minute closes were all in the mid-73k range.
CoinGecko and Coinbase spot checks also showed high-73k pricing.
timing_relevance: High: the contract resolves on the Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on 2026-04-15, so exact date, timezone, and minute mapping matter.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact handling of any venue-display or post-hoc data edge case was not independently documented beyond the rule text.
No full volatility/event calendar audit was performed beyond current price verification.
what_would_change_view: A move back toward 72k, visible Binance divergence from other venues, or evidence of settlement-minute ambiguity would lower the Yes estimate materially.
